text:
Market today: The mood is mixed for tech. Jamie Dimon points to booming markets and a strong consumer, but IBM's 23% drop after an earnings warning is a direct hit to the tech sector. Fed policy comments about inflation could also drive market sentiment, especially for growth stocks.

Your holdings: SOXL (-28.18%) and SPAL (-30.34%) are both leveraged ETFs marked "SELL" with significant losses. These need immediate attention, especially with margin amplifying drops. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec with a notable loss.

Watch: Keep an eye on how IBM's warning impacts other tech/chip names. Also, monitor any further Fed commentary on inflation and rate policy.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged, underperforming positions, given your margin exposure.
plays: SOXL & SPAL: These leveraged "SELL" positions are deeply negative; consider trimming or setting tighter stops below current prices to manage margin risk.
NVDA: Scanner shows a "WATCH" setup (9/12) for this chip name in daily discount; consider entry 205.12-204.45 with stop 203.01.
